title: Starting $(podman quadlet) on a fresh debian-13 vm reports "Error: unrecognized command `podman quadlet`"
body:
### Issue Description

We had a workshop at devconf.cz.

Debian-13 (trixie) packaging does not support $(podman quadlet)

Compiling the binary with libsystemd-dev enables $(podman quadlet), but something on the background is not properly set up.

 * There is no instruction on what to install on Debian to compile podman
 * There is no package that provides podman-machine dependencies.
 * There is not podman-quadlet in 5.4.2 in Debians package.



### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1.
2.
3.


### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected

### podman info output

```yaml
If you are unable to run podman info for any reason, please provide the podman version, operating system and its version and the architecture you are running.
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body: @inknos we talked in person about the issue.

> There is no instruction on what to install on Debian to compile podman

it is not well maintained but there is https://podman.io/docs/installation#build-and-run-dependencies

That should have the build deps including libsystemd-dev listed.
We could properly need someone would keep this updated and maintained but since it is hosted on podman.io that should likely be tracked as issue there.


> There is no package that provides podman-machine dependencies.

Well that is a debian packaging problem and would need to be reported against the debian package not upstream


> There is not podman-quadlet in 5.4.2 in Debians package.

Now as for debain packages well that is just how debian stable works by not updating versions so old podman versions that miss a lot of new features (and are not supported by upstream) are to be expected there.

> > There is no instruction on what to install on Debian to compile podman
>
> it is not well maintained but there is https://podman.io/docs/installation#build-and-run-dependencies

@marcusdots would you check if the install instructions are correct? then we can go ahead and fix podman.io docs. we are aware of the docs being out of sync sometimes so we could use your help.


> >There is no package that provides podman-machine dependencies.
>
> Well that is a debian packaging problem and would need to be reported against the debian package not upstream

this is the leas priority imho since podman-machine should not be necessary on linux and the way Qemu is packaged and distributed my vary if I am not mistaken.



> > There is not podman-quadlet in 5.4.2 in Debians package.
>
> Now as for debain packages well that is just how debian stable works by not updating versions so old podman versions that miss a lot of new features (and are not supported by upstream) are to be expected there.

@marcusdots , when we talked in person you mentioned you could bring into the conversation more debian devs, I'd be interested in connecting and ship a podman version closer to upstream. however, I am not aware of debian's policies and updates, so that could be never fixed on distro side.

> @marcusdots , when we talked in person you mentioned you could bring into the conversation more debian devs, I'd be interested in connecting and ship a podman version closer to upstream. however, I am not aware of debian's policies and updates, so that could be never fixed on distro side.


Well I am sure @siretart (the podman debian maintainer) would appreciate help. But since this is about debian packaging it is likely best to talk directly with him and not via us upstream, I am happy to provide general guidance on packing as upstream dev of course and have done so many times in the past.

Generally speaking he does a good job keeping podman updated, but those updates happen in debian sid/testing not stable, see https://tracker.debian.org/pkg/podman
As for stable updates I am not a debian packager but AFAIK the official policy is just to add security fixes https://www.debian.org/doc/manuals/debian-faq/getting-debian.en.html#updatestable

There is the backport repo concept which can be used to get newer builds into stable but AFAIK that is extra work to maintain and with the amount of dependencies podman has, both binaries ( i.e. crun. conmon, netavark,etc...) and source (all the go deps, debian policies do not allow the user of our vendor/ deps AFAIK).

This sounds indeed like a Debian specific packaging issue. Please file it using https://bugs.debian.org

Can you confirm that the "sid" or "testing" package work properly? I notice that the trixie package indeed ships a libexec/quadlet binary: https://packages.debian.org/trixie/amd64/podman/filelist -- I'm not sure I totally follow what's exactly broken here.

> There is no package that provides podman-machine dependencies.

that's partly correct. There was a packaging bug that was reported as
https://bugs.debian.org/cgi-bin/bugreport.cgi?bug=1139170 and fixed in debian/sid.
any chance you could install "debian/testing" in a VM and test the quadlet behavior there? -- I'd expect it to work, but please let me know if anything is missing, preferably at bugs.debian.org
